title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. violaceum
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. violaceum, also known as the Alpine Rose or Rock Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a low-growing, woody perennial that is native to the Mediterranean region and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic. Helianthemum apenninum subsp. violaceum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a versatile and effective natural remedy.
